clinical_history: Young adult male with hyperthyroidism and weakness.  The ECG is most consistent with which ONE of the following abnormalities?
diagnosis: The patient's serum potassium was 1.9 mEq/L at about the time this ECG was recorded.  Resting sinus tachycardia with PR prolongation is present.  The P waves in lead II are superimposed on the TU waves.  Marked prolongation of repolarization is present with very large U waves (V3-V4, especially).  The  patient was diagnosed with thyrotoxic periodic paralysis (TPP) with hypokalemia, an important syndrome that has occasioned increasing interest due to the its emerging endocrine, molecular and genomic bases.
